rationale: Spiller et al. (2013) is cited as the explicit methodological basis for the floodlight analyses used to probe the paper's interaction effects. The citing paper reports Johnson-Neyman style significance regions and cutoff values (e.g., SBC thresholds) in multiple studies using this approach. This is not a passing literature-list citation, because the cited work directly informs how central hypotheses are tested and interpreted, even though the method is applied rather than theoretically advanced.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al.'s moderated-regression probing framework, especially floodlight analysis, to interpret conditional effects in its studies. It uses that method to determine where self-improvement effects on brand judgments are significant across moderator values.
